core_purpose: Afterburner is an open-source presentation-layer dashboard built with Astro and React that lets users paste a YouTube URL and view AI-generated viral Twitter threads in a styled grid. The actual scraping and LLM compute is intended to be delegated to an external Scramjet workflow via a webhook, while this repo only ships the UI shell and a stub API route.

main_features.name: Dashboard UI with YouTube URL input and threads grid
Scramjet webhook integration for real scraping/LLM compute
Dark-themed marketing layout with OpenGraph meta

technical_debt: No tests exist (no test runner, no test files). The /api/generate route is a hardcoded mock with a setTimeout delay rather than a real Scramjet webhook call, so the headline feature is non-functional out of the box. README contains an unresolved TODO placeholder for the demo GIF (.github/assets/demo.webp). The repo carries both Biome and Prettier+ESLint configs (biome.json untracked, lint-staged still uses prettier) which is a redundant/inconsistent tooling setup. CI status badge is referenced but no .github/workflows/ci.yml is visible in the tree. The hq/ directory (architecture.json, fleet.yaml) is untracked and unintegrated.
active_roadmap: No open GitHub issues found — roadmap is implicit: wire /api/generate to a real Scramjet webhook, add the demo.webp asset, consolidate Biome vs Prettier/ESLint tooling, and add a CI workflow to back the badge.
